lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Damaged roof tiles for sale'... 'Come on down to Bob's Axes'... 'Contributions welcomed to Ned's New-Window Fund'...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait a minute, what's this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This could be worth looking into.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, let's go and see what Sarah has in mind.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Hello. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've come about the bounty.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Oh thank Saradomin! It's about time someone responded, something needs to be done!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you explain what happened?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Of course. I was making my monthly delivery to The Sheared Ram in Lumbridge, heading east near the Draynor Village crossroads.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Suddenly, out of the trees came a hulking monster! It chased me for a short distance, but luckily Rosie managed to spook it enough to give me a headstart.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like it must have given you quite the scare!

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: It certainly did. I even lost my cart full of farm produce!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did this monster look like?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: I didn't get a very good look at it I'm afraid. It was getting dark and as soon as I saw it charging at me, I ran.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: I can still hear the sound, though. A horrible grunting... squealing...

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Is there anything else you wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any tips on finding this thing?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Tips...? You're supposed to be the professional monster slayer!

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Although, I suppose a good place to start would be checking the area I was attacked. See if you can find the remains of my cart.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, yes that could work.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Is there anything else you wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Whereabouts were you attacked again?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: I remember being somewhere near the crossroads, north of Draynor Village.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Sorry that I'm not much help. It happened so fast, the whole thing is a bit of a blur.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's okay. Crossroads north of Draynor... At least I have somewhere to start.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Is there anything else you wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mentioned that Rosie spooked the monster. Who's that?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Oh, that'll be our lady over there by the fire. She's such a good dog, always accompanies me on deliveries.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: You can pet her if you like. She's very friendly.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Is there anything else you wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think that'll be all for now.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Excellent! Best of luck to you, I'll be waiting right here with your reward.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Hello. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Weird... What sort of monster charges at someone and then steals a bunch of carrots?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you look at that... Apparently our monster is picky.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something pretty big must have come running through here to knock this over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This looks like the place. It's a long way down, though... I'll need to tie something onto the edge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh... there's a rope already down here. I hope noone else has got to this monster first.

sections.title: Talking to Sarah after finding the cave

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Hello. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Oh it's you, my monster slayer! How goes the quest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it... I've managed to find its lair though.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Sounds like you're making progress. Take care!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's also a scrawled note. It's barely readable...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's not exactly reassuring.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uhh... Nice piggy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Argh! My eyes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Argh! My eyes! The goggles do nothing!

lines.speaker: Pig Thing
lines.text: *Sniff* *Sniff*

lines.speaker: Pig Thing
lines.text: *Snort*

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Well then, what do we have here?

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Still breathing, eh...?

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Seems I came at just the right time.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: We'd better get you out of here before that thing returns.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: W-what happened? Where am I?

sections.title: Attempting to enter the Sourhog cave after being attacked by the Sourhog

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I find out more about that monster from Spria in Draynor Village before heading back down there.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Oh, you're awake!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you? How did I end up here?

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: My name's Spria and I'm a slayer master. You've had a nasty encounter with a Sourhog.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A Sourhog? You mean the giant pig monster?

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Precisely. You're extremely lucky that I found you. Another few hours and you'd have been hog chow.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Were you responding to Sarah's bounty too?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes... It sounded like an easy few coins...

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: I thought as much. I found you on the floor of the cave, you must have been knocked unconscious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That thing caught me completely off-guard. Normally I can handle myself in a fight, but its spitting attack is pretty fearsome!

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: I have an item for you that will help you on your quest, but you don't seem to have enough space. Come and see me again when you've cleared some room.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: The beast's saliva is extremely acidic and your eyes are most vulnerable. It'll cause terrible injuries and leave you unable to attack or defend yourself very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how am I supposed to handle that?

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Luckily, the Slayer skill provides us with an array of useful equipment and techniques for dealing with... special foes.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: In the case of our Sourhog, you'll be needing these.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: This eyewear will protect you from the spitting attack, allowing you to fight the monster on equal terms.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Most enemies have a weakness, too. The Sourhog's thick hide provides excellent protection against slashing and insulates it from magic damage.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: You'd be best trying to pierce it with arrows or a stabbing weapon.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Go now, the bounty is all yours to claim. I suggest you recoup your strength and head back to fight it using your new goggles.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: If you make it out alive, collect your bounty and then come see me so that we can speak further.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you helping me?

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Well, I suppose I could have left you in there to be eaten...

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: But you know what that'd make me? A monster. And as a slayer master, that's really not a great look.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Besides, my father always taught me that knowledge is worth more than gold. I'm happy to sacrifice a few coins in order to pass my experience on to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I sure am glad you were there to lend a hand. Thank you!

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Don't mention it. Was there something else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did I end up back in Draynor Village?

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: I carried you out and dragged you back here to my house to recover, it really wasn't easy. You're welcome to rest here for as long as you need.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Was there something else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You said you were a slayer master. What's that?

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: There are many of us. We're experts in the ancient art of monster slaying. People from across Gielinor come to us for tasks and advice.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Well... actually, I'm fairly new to this. I've been learning from my father, Turael, for many years. We decided it was finally time for me to continue the family profession.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Was there something else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have another pair of Reinforced Goggles?

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: No, you're still wearing the last pair I gave you!

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Was there something else?

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: You've still got the last one I gave you, it's there in your inventory! Those things aren't cheap, you know.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Was there something else?

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: I know many people all across Gielinor, and my sources tell me you still have the last one in your bank.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Was there something else?

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Sure, I have another going spare. Please try to take better care of these.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Was there something else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about the Sourhog?

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Well, I'm not much of a history buff... But I'll tell you what I know.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Many years back, before even I was around, there were two rival family farms not far from where we're standing.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Every year the village would hold a 'Finest Swine in Show' prize at the annual faire.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: The same farmer would win year after year without fail. Driven mad with jealousy, his rival decided to hire a dodgy dark wizard to give him an edge over the competition.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: The wizard attempted to alter the pig's appearance. I'm sure you can guess how well that went!

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Long story short, the mutant, what we now know as a Sourhog, went on a brief rampage and escaped.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: It must have somehow managed to reproduce, because people have reported seeing them in groups.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Up until now, so long as they were left alone, they'd keep to themselves. Rumors are, they live in deep underground caverns, scavenging what they can find.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: But if they're living so close-by and attacking people on roads, then... they must be getting bold and trying to resurface.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Was there something else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That'll be all for now.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Very well. Good luck, slayer.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: How goes it?

sections.title: Attempting to enter the hole without reinforced goggles equipped

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't want to go back down there without wearing the right equipment!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think Spria will be rescuing me this time. If I go in there, I'm fighting this thing to the death!

sections.title: Attempting to climb the rope after killing the Sourhog but before severing its foot

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Before I leave, I should go and collect a foot from that dead Sourhog as proof of my kill.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I should go and get something sharp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think that's sharp enough to cut a foot off...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've already collected a foot from this thing. I should take it to Sarah.

sections.title: Attempting to re-enter the Sourhog Cave with a Sourhog foot in the player's inventory

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't need to go back down there right now. I should take the foot to Sarah and collect my reward.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did it! I killed the Sourhog!

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Nicely done. Did you collect your reward from Sarah?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Go and collect your reward and then come and speak with me.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Hello. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That monster certainly won't be troubling you from now on.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Oh that's fantastic news! I'm so relie-

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Wait, how do I know you're not just saying that to get the bounty?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I'll go and get you some proof.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about this as proof?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Eugh! Yes, that certainly looks about right!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed, the smell is... something quite special.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: I'll probably just give it to the dog.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: I'm glad you made it back. Some strange woman in a hood came asking about the job shortly after you left. I didn't really want her back in here.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Anyway, thank you so much! Here's your reward.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I should speak with Spria and tell her that the monster's been dealt with.

sections.title: Talking to Sarah before telling Spria about the bounty completion

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Hello. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Feeling better now that the monster's gone?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Very much so! Thanks again. What was it, anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was a Sourhog. A giant bipedal pig creature!

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Oh crumbs... I think I've heard of those... My grandfather was always rambling on about them.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Nevermind... that's all history now. Weren't you heading off to see Spria?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I suppose I should get going.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Safe travels!

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: 'Ello, and what are you after then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did it! I killed the Sourhog and collected the bounty from Sarah.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Very impressive, slayer. I'm glad that the goggles served you well.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Although, these Sourhogs often live in primitive tribes. I fear that where there's one, there's likely to be more to follow.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: We may have to monitor that lair from time to time to keep their population in check.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: I've noticed that you've unlocked the ability to craft slayer helms. From now on, when assembling one, fasten a pair of Reinforced Goggles to the eye holes to gain their benefit.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: While we're on the topic, I can upgrade any of your existing slayer helms now.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: That should do the trick.

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: By the way, you seem to have a natural talent for killing monsters. Would you like a new slayer task now?

lines.speaker: Spria
lines.text: Very well, come back if you change your mind.
